_comment: v1.2 P3 §5.43 — Lighthouse CI performance budget. Run via `npx @lhci/cli autorun` once the dep lands. Thresholds are intentionally set slightly looser than Google's 'good' targets so the first CI run has headroom; tighten as the app improves. paired with size-limit (declared in package.json), this is the perf regression tripwire the audit asked for.

collect._comment: Lighthouse points at the live Vercel deployment instead of a CI-booted next-start. v1.14.0 tried local boot with explicit startServer config; that fixed the timing race but a prod-build /login still returned 500 in CI (likely Sentry/PostHog init against stub env). The Vercel deployment is the same code, the same prod build, with real env wired up — and it's already part of the verify.sh deploy gate. So we test it directly. Trade-off: the LH job becomes a measurement against the *previous* main commit (Vercel hasn't deployed the just-pushed commit yet), but for perf-budget regression detection that's still useful. /login and /signup live in (auth) and render without auth; if a real (marketing) landing page lands later, add it here.
